meta.purpose: Terminology boundaries, traps, and self-checks for Frontend Motion System.
meta.load_condition: Load with Core for any generation, review, or refactor task in this domain.

terminology.definition: A named, version-controlled value that represents a design decision — applied to motion as duration, easing, and distance scales.
A reusable animation function or component that consumes tokens and produces consistent enter, exit, or attention behavior.
A defined range of values for a motion property: e.g., duration-sm (75ms), duration-md (150ms), duration-lg (300ms), duration-xl (500ms).
Naming primitives by user-facing intent (fade-enter, slide-exit, pulse-attention) rather than implementation (opacity-400ms, translateY-20px).

banned_terms.why: Unspecified CSS transitions create inconsistent timing across components. Every transition should reference a motion token or primitive.
Mockups cannot convey timing, easing, or interaction feedback. Motion decisions must be made in code with live previews, not in static design tools.
Without a duration scale, 'fine' becomes 200ms in one component and 600ms in another. Define the scale first and pick from it.

misunderstandings.claim: Motion tokens limit creativity and produce boring, uniform animation.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Tokens define constraints, not outcomes. A limited palette of durations and easings combined with semantic naming produces more creative and coherent results than unlimited one-off values.

misunderstandings.claim: Copying animation code between components is fine for consistency.
misunderstandings.key_distinction: Copy-pasted code diverges over time and becomes a maintenance burden. Extract the behavior into a named primitive that stays in sync.

self_check.question: Are duration, easing, and distance tokens defined and version-controlled?
Do shared motion primitives cover the most common animation patterns in the product?
Can designers preview every motion primitive without running the full application?
Does CI enforce that new animations use primitives or tokens rather than raw values?
